selected_important_turn.text: I really need food too so if I can have 2 packages of food you can have 1 package of firewood and all 3 of the water.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the first substantive counter-offer by YOU and sets a competitive anchor early. It frames the bargaining as zero-sum and establishes a pattern of claiming high-value items rather than exploring tradeoffs.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although reasonable to express need for food, the turn is purely positional (demanding 2 food) and gives no justification beyond 'need' or any inquiry into the opponent's priorities. That narrows opportunities for value-creating trades and risks hardening the opponent's stance.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the anchor and open integrative options: state the need for food but invite the other's priorities or suggest multiple package options (e.g., "I need most of the food — could we consider splits where I take 2 food if I give up X?"), or propose explicit trade-offs across items rather than a fixed allocation.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (by enabling trade-off thinking rather than fixed zero-sum demands)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (by acknowledging trade-offs and seeking reciprocal concessions)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (more likely to keep the other engaged and avoid escalation)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (less adversarial tone, more collaborative posture)

value2reason.Low: We can survive even without this. Though if we get, it will be good for extra security.
value2reason.Medium: In case of emergency.Also, our basic supplies may decrease.
value2reason.High: To be aware of decreasing food and it may be needed in case of any emergency.

value2reason.Low: I already have a sufficient amount of water.
value2reason.Medium: Where I'm camping it'll be colder so I need to make a bigger fire.
value2reason.High: I have more people so I need to feed them.

dialogue.text: Hi, I'd like to take all of the food and 2 of fire wood. You can have the rest.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I really need food too so if I can have 2 packages of food you can have 1 package of firewood and all 3 of the water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with 2 food, 1 firewood, and all 3 water. Let’s confirm the split: you get 2 food, 1 firewood, 3 water; I get 1 food, 2 firewood, 0 water. That covers all 9 packages.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need most of the food, but I’m open to trade-offs. How about if I take 2 food, you take 1 food, and we split the firewood and water differently? What works for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
